data.title: South Korea's Democratic Party plans to push forward stablecoin legislation after the June election.
data.description: Kim Hyun-jung, a member of the Democratic Party of Korea's Digital Assets Task Force, stated that they plan to submit a basic law on stablecoins and digital assets (the second phase of legislation) after the June local elections, and simultaneously advance the bill's subcommittee review. He pointed out that disagreements remain on issues such as restrictions on major shareholders' holdings in exchanges, requiring further discussion. He also believes that given the positive attitude of Bank of Korea Governor Shin Hyun-song towards stablecoins, the legislation is likely to be passed. Furthermore, the task force is considering meeting with him after the elections to advance the design of a CBDC and a Korean won stablecoin system. (Edaily)

data.title: JST Tokens Burned to Enhance Scarcity and Value
data.description: According to TRONSCAN data, the JST treasury address has been consistently withdrawing funds from protocol earnings and transferring them to a burn address for permanent destruction. According to ChainCatcher, this process has resulted in the cumulative burning of over 1.356 billion JST tokens, accounting for 13.7% of the total supply. All transactions are publicly verifiable on the blockchain, and the ongoing deflation driven by real earnings is enhancing the scarcity of JST tokens. This development is expected to provide long-term support for the token's value, marking a new phase of value growth driven by intrinsic profits and transparent mechanisms.

data.title: Optimism Ecosystem Introduces Enterprise Privacy Solution
data.description:
Sunnyside Labs, a key development team within the Optimism ecosystem, has launched an enterprise-grade privacy solution called Privacy Boost on the OP Mainnet. According to Foresight News, this solution is designed to meet compliance requirements and utilizes a hybrid ZK+TEE architecture, achieving proof generation speeds of under 500 milliseconds. Privacy Boost is a plug-and-play SDK that facilitates privacy computation on any OP Stack chain.

Sunnyside Labs specializes in blockchain infrastructure and has collaborated with OP Labs and other core contributors for over three years to develop the core infrastructure of the OP Stack.

data.title: Malaysia's Economic Committee to Address Unemployment Due to Energy Crisis Next Week
data.description: Malaysia's Ministry of Communications has announced that the country's Economic Committee will convene next week to discuss the unemployment issues arising from the ongoing energy crisis. According to Jin10, the meeting aims to address the economic challenges and explore potential solutions to mitigate the impact on the workforce. The energy crisis has led to significant disruptions in various sectors, contributing to rising unemployment rates. The committee's discussions will focus on strategies to stabilize the job market and support affected industries.

data.title: Mastercard joins the Blockchain Security Standards Committee to develop a security framework with Coinbase and others.
data.description: Mastercard has joined the Blockchain Security Standards Committee (BSSC) as an Articles of Association member, and will work with organizations such as Coinbase, Fireblocks, Anchorage Digital, and BitGo to develop a security framework for blockchain networks and tokenized assets. Mastercard will also participate in working groups related to security and privacy guidelines, and Claire Le Gal, head of Mastercard's security solutions division, will represent the company on the BSSC board. The BSSC stated that Mastercard's experience in the payments sector will help improve blockchain security rules. (BeInCrypto)

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Praises Jim Hanson's Explanation of America's 'Victory'
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump commended Jim Hanson for his insightful explanation of America's 'victory' during a prime-time segment on Fox News. According to Jin10, Trump highlighted Hanson's ability to articulate the nation's achievements in the ongoing conflict, emphasizing the significance of his analysis.

data.title: Chainlink Achieves Key Security Certifications
data.description: Chainlink has announced that Deloitte & Touche LLP, one of the Big Four accounting firms, has completed a SOC 2 Type 2 audit of Chainlink's CCIP and data feeds. According to Foresight News, Chainlink is now the only data and interoperability oracle platform that meets critical institutional security standards, having obtained SOC 2 Type 2, SOC 2 Type 1, and ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certifications.

data.title: Global Markets Face Uncertainty Amid Political and Economic Shifts
data.description:
On April 22, Kevin Warsh criticized the U.S. Federal Reserve during a hearing, highlighting its 'lost direction' in attempting to reshape policy frameworks while avoiding key political issues. According to BlockBeats, this has led the market to realize that future monetary policy may involve political risks, affecting interest rates as stable pricing anchors.

Meanwhile, the Middle East situation appears to be cooling but has entered a more challenging phase for pricing. U.S. President Donald Trump extended the ceasefire while maintaining blockades, effectively transforming war into economic suppression. Iran's refusal to negotiate while maintaining readiness indicates that the conflict persists in a different form. Consequently, energy has become a manipulable tool, and inflation is no longer solely under central bank control.

Japan's response to energy and exchange rate pressures has delayed its original interest rate hike schedule, revealing a global issue of 'policy desynchronization.' As major central banks struggle to operate in sync, funds lack a unified direction, becoming dispersed and awaiting clearer signals.

These developments indicate a shift from an era of predictable policies to one of 'credit fragmentation.' The current market lacks trends, showing only volatility and shifts without consensus. Prices are repeatedly redefined rather than continuously changing.

The key question is whether funds trust policies or the inherent risks. If the market regains trust in central banks, liquidity will return, supporting risk assets. Conversely, if the focus shifts to conflict and politics, funds will withdraw, increasing volatility. A prolonged tug-of-war between these forces results in the current cycle of false breakouts and market churn.

The market's future direction depends on three signals being simultaneously corrected: the Federal Reserve's ability to rebuild unquestionable policy credibility, substantive rather than verbal easing in Middle Eastern energy channels, and major central banks returning to predictable policy rhythms. Until these conditions are met, any market movement remains a temporary choice amid uncertainty.

data.title: Iran Undecided on New Talks with U.S.
data.description:
Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson, Baghaei, announced on April 22 that Iran has not yet decided whether to engage in a new round of negotiations with the United States. According to BlockBeats, Baghaei stated in a media interview that Iran participated in the initial talks with sincerity and seriousness, but the U.S. displayed a lack of commitment and inconsistent positions.

Baghaei emphasized that Iran has clearly communicated its stance, indicating that if attending negotiations in Islamabad, Pakistan, aligns with national interests, Iran will participate. However, no decision has been made at this time. He criticized the U.S. for its blockade of Iranian ports and actions against Iranian merchant ships, describing these as aggressive moves unbefitting a nation genuinely committed to a serious diplomatic process.

data.title: Turkey's Finance Minister Simsek: Recent Developments to Impact Inflation, But Downward Trend to Persist
data.description: Turkey's Finance Minister Simsek stated that recent developments, specifically the Iran conflict, are expected to influence Turkey's inflation. According to Jin10, Simsek emphasized that despite these impacts, the overall downward trend in inflation will remain unchanged. The minister's comments come amid ongoing geopolitical tensions, which have raised concerns about economic stability in the region. Simsek reassured that the Turkish government is committed to maintaining its economic policies to ensure inflation continues on its downward trajectory.

data.title: Iran: First round of talks showed sincerity, but the US remained indifferent.
data.description: Today (April 22), Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Baghae stated that Iran has not yet decided whether to participate in the new round of negotiations with the United States. In a media interview, Baghae said that Iran participated in the first round of negotiations with the United States in good faith and with a serious attitude, but the US side showed a cold attitude and lacked sincerity, with its position being inconsistent. He stated that Iran has clearly indicated that if going to Islamabad, Pakistan to participate in the negotiations is in its national interest, it will participate, but no decision has been made yet. Baghae stated that the US blockade of Iranian ports is an act of aggression, and the shooting and seizure of Iranian merchant ships is not the behavior of a country truly committed to a serious diplomatic process. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iranian Foreign Ministry: No decision yet made on whether to participate in a new round of negotiations with the United States
data.description: On the 22nd local time, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Bagaei stated that Iran has not yet decided whether to participate in a new round of negotiations with the United States. (CCTV News)

data.title: South African Inflation Rises Amid Global Tensions
data.description: South Africa's annual inflation rate increased in March, Bloomberg posted on X, reflecting economic pressures even before the onset of the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, which has led to a spike in domestic fuel prices. The inflation uptick highlights the country's vulnerability to international geopolitical events, impacting local economic conditions.

data.title: KCEX Continues on South Korea's iOS App Store Amid Regulatory Challenges
data.description: KCEX, an overseas cryptocurrency exchange, remains accessible on South Korea's iOS App Store despite regulations aimed at unregistered virtual asset service providers. According to NS3.AI, industry sources revealed that South Korean authorities had identified the exchange for unregistered business activities as early as last August. This situation underscores the limitations of the blocking measures implemented by South Korean financial authorities and investigative agencies.

data.title: Yi Lihua: The current rebound is weak, exhibiting characteristics of a bear market rally.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua stated that while the current cryptocurrency market rebound continues, its overall performance remains weaker compared to the S&P 500's record highs and MicroStrategy's continued buying, exhibiting characteristics of a bear market rebound. He pointed out that MicroStrategy's aggressive fundraising for Bitcoin purchases may be effective in the short term, but faces uncertainty in the medium term as macroeconomic and financial risks escalate. However, he believes there is no need for pessimism, as opportunities often arise during bear markets.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Rebound Analyzed Amid Macro Financial Risks
data.description:
On April 22, Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua shared insights on the ongoing rebound in the cryptocurrency market. According to BlockBeats, Yi noted that while discussions about the rebound are prevalent, the current recovery appears weak compared to the new highs in the S&P 500 and MicroStrategy's aggressive buying.

Yi highlighted that MicroStrategy's strategy of aggressive financing to purchase cryptocurrencies seems effective in the short term but poses potential risks in the medium term, especially during macroeconomic financial uncertainties. He emphasized the importance of being prepared for significant opportunities and maintained that there is no need for pessimism in the crypto market. Opportunities often arise during bear markets, while bull markets are typically when investors enter happily but may incur losses.

data.title: China Expresses Satisfaction Over Taiwan President's Blocked Trip
data.description: China has expressed its approval of three countries that reportedly prevented Taiwan President Lai Ching-te from visiting an African diplomatic ally. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting China's diplomatic maneuvers in the region. The move is seen as part of China's ongoing efforts to isolate Taiwan on the international stage, reinforcing its stance on the One China policy. The details of the countries involved and the specific diplomatic ally were not disclosed, but the incident underscores the complex geopolitical tensions surrounding Taiwan's international relations.

data.title: A whale deposited 9,500 wstETH into Spark and borrowed 9,500 ETH to transfer to Binance for sale.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, whale 0xD91D created a new address and deposited 9,500 wstETH into Spark. It then lent out the 9,500 ETH, worth approximately $22.68 million, and transferred it to Binance for sale. Simultaneously, it withdrew USDE from Binance to repay its loan on Aave.

data.title: Another cargo ship has been shelled; no casualties reported.
data.description: The UK Maritime Trade Operations Office has received a report of an incident 8 nautical miles west of Iran. The captain of a departing cargo ship reported being shelled and is currently at rest. All crew members are safe and present. No damage to the vessel has been reported. Increased activity in the Strait of Hormuz is noted, and vessels are encouraged to report any suspicious activity. (Previously, a container ship reported being shelled by Iranian armed forces.) (Jinshi)

data.title: Nordea Bank Traders Caught Off Guard by Market Shift Due to Iran Conflict
data.description: Nordea Bank's rates traders faced unexpected challenges in March as the Iran conflict led to a swift shift in market expectations for monetary policies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how the geopolitical tensions impacted financial markets, causing traders to reassess their strategies. The sudden change in the market outlook underscored the volatility and unpredictability that geopolitical events can introduce into financial markets. Nordea Bank's experience serves as a reminder of the complexities involved in navigating global economic landscapes influenced by international conflicts.
